text_blocks.snippet: Reunification counselling services in Vancouver, BC, are designed to help families, children, and youth rebuild relationships following separations, child protection involvement, or high-conflict divorces. These services often include therapeutic, in-home, and in-office support, with many programs funded by the Ministry of Children and Family Development (MCFD).
Key Providers and Programs in Vancouver/BC

list.snippet: Family Services of Greater Vancouver (FSGV): Offers Family Preservation and Reunification Services. This program helps families with children who are at risk of out-of-home placement or are in the process of reunifying. Services include individual/group counselling, parenting education, and therapeutic supervised visits, typically requiring a referral from an MCFD social worker.

Westcoast Family Centres Society: Provides intensive in-home and office-based counseling for families in crisis, focusing on child protection, blended families, and strengthening parent-child relationships.

Reconnect Families: Specializes in reunification for families experiencing conflict, alienation, or child resistance to visitation due to separation/divorce. They offer a team approach with registered clinical counsellors, intensive weekends, and in-home outreach.

Bright Star Counselling (Port Moody/Metro Vancouver): Offers a reunification program for families with distance between parent and child, often utilizing play-based, in-person sessions to rebuild healthy, secure attachments.

REACH Reunification Program (Dr. Susan Gamache): Focuses on high-conflict, alienated, or estranged relationships, providing in-depth, team-based therapeutic support for families, including court-involved cases.
Touchstone Family Association: Provides specialized family preservation and reunification services, including crisis intervention and parent-teen mediation.
WATARI Counselling Services: Offers one-to-one, activity-based support for high-risk youth and individuals to reconnect with community resources. Bright Star Counselling +8
MCFD Referrals: Many of the most intensive, no-fee programs (like FSGV or Westcoast Family Centres) require a referral from a Ministry of Children and Family Development (MCFD) social worker.
Private/Specialized Services: Programs like Reconnect Families or Bright Star Counselling can be contacted directly for private or retained services. Bright Star Counselling +4
Child Protection Concerns: Addressing abuse, neglect, or high-risk situations.
High-Conflict Divorce/Alienation: Rebuilding trust when relationships are strained or broken.
Parenting Skills & Education: Teaching child development, communication, and conflict resolution.
Indigenous Family Support: Culturally sensitive services, with specific Indigenous Family Preservation Workers available in certain programs. 211 British Columbia +6
